macro_events.event_summary: US-Iran tensions escalate, lifting the Dow, while Palantir slides after its earnings report.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major; Iran tensions raise Brent risk premium, directly lifting upstream realizations. Mideast supply disruption is the most direct transmission.
Same mechanism as XOM — leveraged to crude price spikes; historically trades tightly with geopolitical risk premium in the Gulf.
High-beta US E&P (Permian-weighted) with strong torque to crude prices; outperforms majors when oil rallies on supply-shock fears.
Prime missile defense contractor (PAC-3, THAAD) — direct beneficiary if Iran tensions escalate to munitions resupply or regional missile defense deployment.
Maker of Patriot/Standard missiles and Tomahawk; direct exposure to Mideast munitions consumption and replenishment contracts.
Strategic deterrence and ISR exposure; defense complex rotates higher together on Iran headlines, with B-21/long-range strike narratives in play.

losers.rationale: Direct headline driver — earnings reaction selling, high-multiple AI name with low margin for guidance disappointment.
C3.ai — peer-rotation correlation; Palantir is the bellwether for enterprise/government AI software, weak print pressures the cohort.
United — high international/long-haul exposure including Mideast overflight rerouting risk plus jet fuel cost sensitivity.
Carnival — bunker fuel cost exposure plus discretionary travel demand sensitivity if tensions escalate to broader regional conflict.

headlines: Stock Market Today: Dow Rises Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions; Palantir Slides After Earnings Report (Live Coverage)
